messages: Did you remember to take your blood pressure medicine?
Mruczek scratched the sofa again. That cat will be the death of me.
Honey, Mrs. Halinka was asking about you. Call her when you have a moment.
Did you hear they're doing something in that forest outside town again? Grandma says there are noises at night. Heavy machinery.
Why aren't you replying? Is everything okay?
I called your office. They said you haven't shown up since Friday. What's going on?

choices.text: Mom, this isn't N. I found her phone. She's missing.
Everything's fine, don't worry. I'll talk to you later.

npcMessages: What?? How do you mean missing?! Who is this?!
God... I'm calling the police. Right now.

choices.text: DON'T call the police! They're involved in this!

npcMessages: What are you saying... involved how...
Fine. I won't call. But tell me what's happening.
Please, tell me my daughter is alive.

choices.text: I'm doing everything I can to find her. Trust me.

npcMessages: Alright... alright. I trust you.
Please, be careful. Whoever you are.
Honey? Why are you writing so weirdly?
This doesn't sound like you. What's going on?

systemMessages.stranger_danger: Honey, there's a strange car in front of the house. Someone is hanging around the windows. Where are you?! Answer me!

messages: I see you've managed to unlock her phone. We don't have much time. They already know you have it.

choices.text: Who are you? Where is the owner of this phone?
This is a joke. I'm going to the police with this.

npcMessages: A friend. And the police are the last place you should go right now if you want her to survive.
The police? Why do you think she had to run? Don't be naive.
You have to trust me. Go to the Photo Gallery. Find a photo taken last night. You must decrypt the message hidden in it before they disable this phone. Hurry.
One more thing — there are documents she gathered in the Files. Invoices, recordings, a bribe list. Read them. You'll understand who you're dealing with.
And don't try calling the police from this phone. You have no signal. Besides... the police are part of the problem.
Now go to Photos. Find that dark forest photo — taken yesterday night. Click on it and press the Info button at the bottom. The code is hidden there.

systemMessages.sheriff_nudge: The Sheriff has reached out. You have a few minutes before he arrives. DO NOT answer him immediately — read the Files and Mail first. The more you know, the better response you'll choose.
systemMessages.plan_b_nudge: One more thing — there is a second locked note, "Plan B". You'll find the code for it in the transcript of the recording in Files. Look for the time. It's important.
systemMessages.journalist_nudge: Good choice. Anita will reach out — answer her. Send everything you have. It's the only chance.
systemMessages.clue_nudge: Good job. See that code in the comment? 7309. Go into Notes — there's a locked note there. Enter that code. Read what N. left behind.
systemMessages.files_nudge: See those invoices? 14 thousand every month. "Consulting". Now you understand why the police aren't looking for N.
systemMessages.browser_nudge: Yes. That was her secret archive. The locker, the prosecutor, the stalking forum — it's all her research. Save those addresses. They might come in handy.
systemMessages.recorder_nudge: Brilliant. Yes — that was them: Anita in the first one, Commander K. in the second, Tomasz B. (HB partner) in the third. Now Anita has her own face on tape. This is gold.
systemMessages.maps_nudge: I have her route. House → office → cafe with Anita at 2 PM → parking at 9 PM → Kabacki Forest C-2 at 11:45 PM. Exactly where they were digging. She didn't disappear alone — she went to check the sector.
systemMessages.email_nudge: You put together her last message. She didn't have time to send this one to Anita. Everything is there: 2 PM meeting, sector C-2, Signal password. Anita doesn't know it herself — you have to pass it on.

messages: Hey N., I've got the material ready for publication. I just need your green light and those documents you mentioned.
The editor-in-chief is hesitating — afraid of a lawsuit from Helion-Bud. Give me anything in black and white and we'll run it on Saturday.
N., I'm starting to worry. Reach out as soon as you can.

choices.text: Anita, it's me. N. is gone. I have all her evidence on Helion-Bud and the commander. Are you taking it?
Anita, are you there? This is urgent, I don't have much time.

npcMessages: Oh God. I knew something happened to her.
Send everything you have. Photos, notes, names. I have a secure channel.

choices.text: Sending: forest photo, N.'s note, mentions of Commander K. and Helion-Bud.
I have nothing, but believe me — it's all true.

npcMessages: Honey, I can't write 'believe me'. The editorial board will fire me.
Come back when you have at least one invoice or recording.
Good luck.
I have everything. Opening the editor-in-chief.
We're going on the front page. It hits the press tomorrow at 6:00 AM.
N. would be proud of you. Find somewhere safe to hide until morning.

choices.text: I don't know what you're talking about, I found this phone in a park.
I know everything about Helion-Bud. Photos and notes are already secured.
It's too late. Everything is on its way to the editorial office.
I can disappear. How much is it worth to you?
How many times have we had this same conversation?

npcMessages: Good joke. My man is already on his way to you. Don't move.
Think you're clever? Take a look behind you.
You're bluffing. You didn't make it in time.
Ah. Smart boy.
50 thousand. Cash. Tomorrow at 6:00 AM at the station.
And you better not have a second copy. Because then you die.
What?
What are you saying?
How do you know?
Regardless. Let me see you.

systemMessages.initial_warning: I know you're digging through that phone. Put it down before I come for it myself.
systemMessages.final_warning: One minute. Either you answer, or I'm coming.

choices.text: Tomasz? The tree that fell on the roof.
Tomasz, I have N.'s phone. Something happened to her.

npcMessages: How do you know that? How do you have her number?
Before I say anything, give me the password we established with each other. Did N. tell you?

choices.text: The tree that fell on the roof.
I don't know any password. Just help me.

npcMessages: That's not how it works. If you don't know the password, you didn't know N.
Hide the phone. Tomorrow buy a new card and leave town.
Good luck.
Fine. So it was true.
Listen carefully - I have everything. Recordings, invoice copies, photos. Everything she gathered plus what I gathered over 2 years after they fired me from HB.
There is only one path that works. Central headquarters. Duty prosecutor. Not county. Not city. CENTRAL.

choices.text: I'm going. Give me 2 hours.
What if central headquarters is also compromised?

npcMessages: It's not. I checked. K. has no connections there.
Besides, you have no other option. Either you go, or this all falls apart after us.

choices.text: Fine. I'm going.

npcMessages: Send me all the evidence immediately via Signal. You have the number in her note.
We'll meet in front of the building. I'll be with the journalist.
The prosecutor is calling me. He says he just got the materials by email. They're moving.
We did it.

systemMessages.initial_warning: I know you have her phone. I saw a light in her window last night — someone was there. It wasn't her. If you know the password, write it. If not — hide the phone and run.

choices.text: I have evidence in the Helion-Bud and Commander K. case. I want to testify officially, under my own name.
I prefer to hand over the materials anonymously. No cameras, no names.
Can I count on witness protection?

npcMessages: Yes, we will provide it. Status of an anonymous state witness or family relocation — depending on what you choose.
But beware: full protection only applies to official witnesses. An anonymous deposit does not grant status.

choices.text: In that case — I will testify officially.
Thank you, but I prefer an anonymous deposit.

npcMessages: Good. Please report at 11:00 AM at the district prosecutor's office building, 28 Chocimska St. The journalist can be present.
Press conference at 11:30 AM. Your testimony will go live. In the afternoon we will detain K. and his people.
Good luck. We meet at 11:00 AM.
I understand. That's cautious — and also effective, though slower.
Send everything to our encrypted address. The case will proceed without your involvement. We will reveal the names of the compromised public only after confirming the materials.
I wish you peace. No one will know about you.

systemMessages.decoded: I have your materials. Everything encrypted. Let's return to the conversation — a decision awaits you.
systemMessages.initial_warning: This is Prosecutor R. from the Internal Affairs Department. Tomasz W. passed on your details. We have a moment — please tell me how you want to play this.

systemMessages.night: Dark at your place, isn't it?
systemMessages.loc_prefix: I know where you are. Don't move.
systemMessages.warning_phone: You shouldn't have turned this on. This phone isn't yours.
systemMessages.last_warning: I'm coming for you. Put the phone down. Last warning.
systemMessages.last_warning_city: I'm coming to ${city}. Put the phone down. Last warning.

recap.chapter1: Chapter 1 is underway — evening, shortly after N.'s disappearance.
recap.chapter2: You're in Chapter 2 — dawn after the night N. went missing.
recap.chapter3: Chapter 3 is underway — Prosecutor R. is waiting for a decision.
recap.notes_secret: You've unlocked N.'s secret note (PIN 7309) and know about the corruption at the county headquarters.
recap.notes_plan_b: You've also unlocked the "Plan B" note (PIN 1422) — you have Tomasz's contact and the authentication password.
recap.files_progress: You've read {count} out of {total} documents in the Files app.
recap.browser_private: You've unlocked the browser's private mode.
recap.puzzle_email: You've assembled N.'s deleted message from five fragments.
recap.puzzle_voices: You've matched voices from recordings to people from contacts.
recap.puzzle_route: You've reconstructed N.'s route from her last day.
recap.puzzle_signal: You've decrypted the Signal password — the channel with the prosecutor is open.
recap.trust_tomasz_high: Tomasz trusted you — he's waiting for the signal.
recap.trust_anita_high: Anita Z. believes in what you have — ready for publication.
recap.trust_mama_low: Mom is mad at you. She doesn't believe your explanations.
recap.empty: You don't remember much — the game is just beginning.

contacts.anita: Signal! Not SMS. Investigative journalist, local newspaper.
contacts.tomasz: Tomasz. No. 14, 2nd floor. Former HB employee. Password: "the tree that fell on the roof". I TRUST HIM.
contacts.szeryf: Commander K. DO NOT ANSWER. DO NOT CALL BACK. He knows who I am. He knows what I have.

wed.title: Selfie — Plac Zbawiciela
wed.subtitle: Meeting a friend?
wed.detail: It was nice to forget everything for a while.

thu.title: Car outside the house
thu.subtitle: Lights off, 2h under the window
thu.detail: Dark sedan. Couldn't see the driver. They've been there since 10 PM. I'm scared.

hints.chapter3_signal: Prosecutor R. is waiting for confirmation. Open Signal on the home screen — the password is a word from N.'s notes plus the time of the first recording.
hints.photos_clue: Go to Photos. Look for a dark photo from the forest — click Info at the bottom.
hints.notes_lock: You have the code 7309 from the photo's metadata. Open Notes — there is a locked note there.
hints.files_progress: There are documents N. gathered in Files. Read them all — these are your hard evidence.
hints.browser_private: Private tabs in the browser are encrypted. You have the password in the notes: cat's name + birth year.
hints.email_fragments: There's an empty Trash in Mail. Fragments of N.'s message are scattered across other apps. Hold your finger on suspicious elements.
hints.recorder_voices: Recordings in the Recorder have anonymous voices. Match the context of the transcript with people from Contacts.
hints.maps_route: There's an option 'Reconstruct last day' in Maps. Arrange N.'s locations by the hours from the Calendar.
hints.generic_ready: You have everything now. Reach out to Anita or Tomasz.

notifications.creepy_camera_body: "Unknown" app obtained access to the camera
notifications.creepy_mic_body: Microphone is active in the background

ngp.caught: Last time they let you get caught. This time don't lie to the Sheriff too quickly.
ngp.escape: Last time you escaped with evidence in your pocket. N. is still out there. Maybe this time we won't leave her there alone?
ngp.truth: Anita published on Saturday. Good. But N. still hasn't returned. Try differently.
ngp.dawn: You already found N. alive once. How did it end? What do you think?
ngp.corruption: You took the envelope. Each of us has our price. Maybe this time you'll try not to have one?
ngp.solitude: Last time you didn't trust anyone. You know Mom wrote to you? Open her messages. This time.
ngp.cycle:
You returned even after I explained everything to you.
Either you're stubborn, or you don't remember.
Or both.
ngp.default: The phone is in your hands again. Do you remember anything from last time? Regardless — we start over.
ngp.meta_closing: I know how it sounds. Just... do it better this time.
ngp.cycle_hint_1: Listen. Did you notice it's always the same phone? Same Friday evening? I did.
ngp.cycle_hint_2: If you ever stop and ask the Sheriff directly — maybe he'll let us both out of here. Try it. Next time he writes to you, you'll get a new option.

prosecutor_bridge.anita: Anita: Anita called the prosecutor earlier than planned. He said he needs to talk to someone who has the materials. You.
prosecutor_bridge.tomasz: Tomasz: The commander has been detained. But the investigators want to talk to you — directly. I'm giving you Prosecutor R.'s number.

about.title: About the game
about.privacy_body:
OFFLINE is a single-player game. We do not collect any personal data.

• Progress and settings are saved exclusively locally on your device.
• Error reports are opt-in and only contain stack trace + version — no identifiers.
about.age_body: Themes: disappearance, corruption, stalking, psychological violence. No physical violence, sexual content, child abuse content.
about.open_source_body:
OFFLINE uses the following open-source libraries:

• Flutter (BSD-3-Clause)
• provider (MIT)
• shared_preferences (BSD-3-Clause)
• audioplayers (MIT)
• google_fonts (Apache-2.0)
• fake_async (BSD-3-Clause)

Full licenses are available in "Open-source Licenses".
about.audio_body: Sounds used in the game come from freesound.org (full license list in assets/audio/README.md) or are procedurally generated under MIT license.
about.contact_body:
For technical and privacy matters: grimssys@gmail.com

© {year} GRIM ORIGIN STUDIO
